message:
fix(indexer): tighten cass#202 self-heal — log after commit, dedupe paths

Self-review pass on ec3d83ec turned up four small but real issues:

1. WARN logged BEFORE the DELETE+commit. If the commit failed, the log
   said "removing N orphan rows" while nothing actually landed on disk.
   Move the log to after `tx.commit()?` so the message reflects what
   actually happened.

2. Double-logging on the success path. `run_index` and
   `cleanup_orphan_fk_rows` both emitted WARN with `total_orphans` /
   `per_table`. Drop the caller-side success log; the function now owns
   the success message (and includes `db_path` for disambiguation across
   concurrent indexers). Caller still logs on Err so the index run does
   not abort silently if the self-heal itself fails.

3. O(n²) lookup in the delete phase: `report.per_table.iter().any(...)`
   per `ORPHAN_FK_TABLES` entry. Build a `SmallVec<[&'static
   OrphanFkTable; 8]>` in the count phase and iterate it directly. With
   five entries this is microseconds either way, but the new shape also
   reads more clearly as "which tables had orphans → delete from each."

4. Test was planting a snippet whose parent message existed (as an
   orphan), so the snippet was removed via FK CASCADE rather than via
   the `snippets` DELETE branch in the function. Plant a directly-
   orphan snippet (`message_id=99999` not in `messages`) so the
   `snippets` DELETE path actually runs. Tightens the assertions from
   `report.total >= 3` to exact `== 4` and verifies per-table counts
   for both `messages` and `snippets`.

Also fix the `if let` collapse clippy lint and a stale comment that
still referenced `token_usage` after the test was switched to snippets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
